data.title: Solana's Price Movement: Key Supply Clusters Absorbed
data.description: Solana (SOL) traded slightly above $84 on April 1. According to NS3.AI, Glassnode data indicated that approximately 37.7 million SOL were acquired between $85.31 and $88.22. A sustained close above $89.82 would signify that the three supply clusters have been absorbed.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Silver Rises Over 1% to $75.92 per Ounce
data.description: Spot silver prices have increased by more than 1% today, currently trading at $75.92 per ounce. According to Jin10, this rise in silver prices reflects ongoing market dynamics and investor sentiment. The precious metal's performance is being closely monitored by traders and analysts as they assess its potential impact on broader market trends.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 69,000 USDT with a 1.71% Increase in 24 Hours

data.title: U.S. Treasury Initiates Implementation of Stablecoin Innovation Act
data.description:
The U.S. Treasury has officially commenced the implementation of the Stablecoin Innovation Act, known as the GENIUS Act, on April 2. According to BlockBeats, the Treasury released a proposed rulemaking notice, marking the first regulation under the act, which spans 87 pages and opens a 60-day public comment period.

Under the GENIUS Act, issuers of payment stablecoins with a total issuance not exceeding $10 billion can opt for state-level regulatory systems, provided that the state's regulatory framework is 'substantially similar' to the federal framework. The U.S. Treasury aims to establish broad principles for determining 'substantial similarity' through this notice.

data.title: Meta to Introduce Dollar-Linked Stablecoin Payments Across Social Platforms by 2026
data.description: Meta is set to launch dollar-linked stablecoin payments on Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp by late 2026. According to NS3.AI, the company plans to collaborate with external partners for infrastructure, compliance, and settlement, rather than issuing its own coin. Meta's focus will be on enhancing user experience and distribution as it integrates stablecoin payments into its social media platforms.

data.title: Elderly Woman in North Carolina Loses $75,000 in Bank Scam
data.description: An elderly woman in Raleigh, North Carolina, fell victim to a scam, losing $75,000 after being deceived by an individual posing as bank support. According to NS3.AI, the scammer convinced her to install a remote-access application, which led to two unauthorized wire transfers. Records indicate that the first transfer amounted to $25,000, followed by a second transfer of $50,000. Despite her efforts, both banks involved informed her that the funds could not be recovered.

data.title: Expert Highlights Governance as Key to Blockchain Longevity Amid Tokenization Growth
data.description: Nilmini Rubin emphasized in an institutional crypto newsletter that governance will be crucial for the sustainability of blockchain networks as tokenization continues to grow. According to NS3.AI, Rubin argues that factors such as speed and token scale are not the sole determinants of a blockchain's endurance. The newsletter also provided a summary of recent developments in stablecoin policy and crypto-backed mortgages.

data.title: U.S. Supreme Court Questions Trump's Birthright Citizenship Efforts
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court has expressed skepticism regarding U.S. President Donald Trump's attempt to revoke birthright citizenship, suggesting that a key part of his immigration plan might be rejected. According to BlockBeats, Trump was present in the front row of the public seating during the oral arguments.

Trump has long argued that the current birthright citizenship system in the United States is heavily abused and is a major loophole in the country's immigration issues. He has publicly stated that birthright citizenship, which grants automatic U.S. citizenship to anyone born on U.S. soil, was not intended as a 'gift' for illegal immigrants or temporary visitors, but was established post-Civil War to protect the descendants of slaves.

PolyBeats reports that on the prediction market Polymarket, the probability of the U.S. Supreme Court overturning Trump's executive order related to birthright citizenship has increased from 81% to 95%.

data.title: Bitcoin mining company Riot Platforms is suspected of selling 500 BTC two hours ago.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Bitcoin mining company Riot Platforms allegedly sold 500 BTC, worth approximately $34.13 million, two hours ago.

data.title: BlackRock withdrew approximately 1450 BTC and 1780 ETH from Coinbase Prime.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, about an hour ago, BlackRock withdrew 1,780 ETH, worth approximately $3.79 million, from a Coinbase Prime address through its Ethereum exchange-traded fund ETHA. Subsequently, BlackRock withdrew a total of approximately 1,450 BTC, worth approximately $99.71 million, from the Coinbase Prime address through its Bitcoin exchange-traded fund IBIT. Further transactions may follow.

data.title: SpaceX to Hold Online Financial Discussion with Banks on May 4
data.description: SpaceX is set to conduct an online financial discussion with several banks on May 4, according to Jin10. The meeting aims to address financial strategies and potential collaborations. This move comes as SpaceX continues to expand its operations and explore new opportunities in the aerospace sector.

data.title: A whale deposited 1,000 BTC that it withdrew yesterday back into Binance; if it sells them, it will make a profit of $1.46 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain's monitoring, a whale deposited 1,000 BTC (US$67.25 million) that it withdrew from Binance yesterday back into Binance. If it sells them, it will make a profit of US$1.46 million.

data.title: Japan and France Strengthen Cooperation on Mineral Supply Chains
data.description:
Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi held talks with visiting French President Emmanuel Macron, reaching an agreement to enhance cooperation on critical mineral supply chains and rare earth procurement. According to RTHK, the leaders also discussed the situation in the Middle East, agreeing on the importance of ensuring safe navigation through the Strait of Hormuz and working towards de-escalation. They confirmed their commitment to closely collaborate on securing oil supplies.

This visit marks Macron's return to Japan since 2023. On the same day, the foreign and defense ministers of both countries conducted a 2+2 meeting, agreeing to strengthen defense cooperation.

data.title: SpaceX Invites Analysts to Visit XAI Data Center in Memphis
data.description: SpaceX has extended an invitation to analysts for a visit to its XAI data center located in Memphis, Tennessee on April 23. According to Jin10, this move is seen as an effort to showcase the capabilities and infrastructure of the data center. The visit is expected to provide insights into SpaceX's operations and technological advancements.

data.title: ProShares UltraShort Bloomberg Crude Oil ETF Sees Record Inflows Despite March Decline
data.description: Investors directed $977 million into the ProShares UltraShort Bloomberg Crude Oil ETF in March, marking the largest single-month inflow for the fund since its inception in 2008. According to NS3.AI, despite this significant inflow, the fund experienced a 41% decline during the same month. Meanwhile, long oil funds such as USO and BNO also reported strong monthly inflows.

data.title: Iran Considers Strait of Hormuz Blockade as Retaliatory Measure
data.description: Iran's Supreme Leader's office has reiterated the possibility of using a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz as a retaliatory measure. According to NS3.AI, details from Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei’s first public speech, shared on social media on April 1, indicate that Iran is also evaluating other potential fronts. The office stated that these fronts would be activated only after a thorough assessment of relevant factors if the conflict persists.

data.title: EdgeX Initiates Buyback Following Token Lockup
data.description: EdgeX has commenced a buyback of its EDGE tokens after previously locking up 140 million tokens, which represents 14% of the total supply. According to NS3.AI, the exchange announced that the earlier lockup is set to last for one year.

data.title: SEC Approves Rule Change for Multi-Crypto Asset Trust Options on NYSE American
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has announced the approval of a rule change application for listing multi-cryptocurrency asset trust options on NYSE American. According to BlockBeats, this marks an expansion from previously allowing options for single cryptocurrency asset trusts to now supporting options for trusts holding multiple crypto assets.

The prerequisites for listing include each cryptocurrency asset held by the trust meeting high liquidity standards, with an average daily market value of at least $700 million over the past 12 months. Additionally, derivative contracts for these assets must have comprehensive surveillance sharing agreements with trading platforms.

Trust shares must adhere to the initial and ongoing listing standards for ETF options on trading platforms and be classified as NMS stocks.

The SEC's review concluded that the rule change will provide investors with greater exposure to cryptocurrency assets and hedging tools without requiring separate SEC approval for each instance, thereby enhancing market efficiency.

data.title: Yesterday, the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 1,752 BTC, and the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 14,488 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, yesterday the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 1,752 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 14,488 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 571 SOL.
